heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 92–60, § 1185 Stat. 163July 29, 1971
label: 1951 to 1958 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 92–60, § 1185 Stat. 163July 29, 1971
notes: Section 1951, acts , ; , , , stated Congressional policy on conversion of sea and saline waters and defined “saline water” and “United States”. See sections 1959 and 1959g(b) and (d) of this title.July 3, 1952, ch. 568, § 166 Stat. 328Pub. L. 87–295, § 175 Stat. 628Sept. 22, 1961

Section 1952, acts , ; , ; , , ; , , ; , –(d), , prescribed duties of Secretary of the Interior relating to (a) research and studies for development of processes and methods for converting saline water; (b) research and technical development work for developing processes and plant designs; reports to Congress; and treatment of demonstration projects as test beds; (c) recommendations to Congress for prototype plant; (d) methods for recovery and marketing of byproducts; and (e) economic studies and surveys on water production costs. See sections 1959a and 1959b(b) of this title.July 3, 1952, ch. 568, § 266 Stat. 328June 29, 1955, ch. 227, § 1(1)69 Stat. 198Pub. L. 87–295, § 175 Stat. 628Pub. L. 89–118, § 1(1)79 Stat. 509Pub. L. 90–30, § 1(b)81 Stat. 78Sept. 22, 1961Aug. 11, 1965June 24, 1967

Section 1954, acts , ; , , , provided for coordination or joint conduct of activities with Department of Defense; cooperation with other Federal agencies; and availability to public of resulting information and developments. See .July 3, 1952, ch. 568, § 466 Stat. 329Pub. L. 87–295, § 175 Stat. 629section 1959d of this titleSept. 22, 1961

Section 1955, acts , ; , , , related to disposal of water and byproducts, disposition of moneys and alteration of existing law. See section 1959d(e) and (f) of this title.July 3, 1952, ch. 568, § 566 Stat. 329Pub. L. 87–295, § 175 Stat. 629Sept. 22, 1961
